provenance: WorldBible section 9: 'Chromatis broadheads punch through three inches of steel' - so steel hardness (7) sits below chromatis (8). Thermal/value numbers invented for v0 balance, needs Eli's blessing.
notes: Best conductor in the set (230, under the 256 cap; conducts better than chromatis 200). meltK deliberately null: a realistic ~1811 K melt would require a 'steel_melt' id outside the v0 vocabulary, and melt => meltsTo is a boot-fail invariant. Flagged as an open question.
